name: Driver: San Francisco
post_apply_message:
Driver San Francisco — fixes applied:
1. Intro Bink files (DriverSplash + ubisoft) renamed to .gfm-orig
2. DXVK's d3d9.dll dropped next to Driver.exe (fixes the SteamOS story-mode crash-to-desktop)
3. Launch wrapper installed at ~/gfm-wrappers/dsf-launch.sh — runs Driver.exe directly (skips the Ubisoft store launcher). Note: the game itself still needs a one-time Ubisoft Connect sign-in — the DRM is in the exe.
4. graphics.ini written to the prefix: 1920x1200 (16:10, Legion Go native) + AntiAliasing ON + RenderQuality high. NOTE: this fixes RESOLUTION + AA only — Driver SF renders 16:9 and STRETCHES to fill 16:10; correcting that stretch/FOV needs an aspect-ratio exe swap, which is left out on purpose (exe swaps break the Uplay DRM). The graphics.ini step is optional and needs the game to have run once (so its Documents folder + prefix exist); if it reports skipped, run the game once then re-apply.
TIP: if the game keeps resetting your resolution, make graphics.ini read-only after applying.
notes: Delisted from Steam Dec 2016, Ubisoft Connect online services shut down Oct 2022 — but the Steam single-player installs still work after a one-time Ubisoft Connect sign-in (the DRM is compiled into Driver.exe; skipping the launcher does not remove it). Fixes bundled: (1) intro skip per PCGW; (2) DXVK d3d9.dll drop = PCGW's documented Linux/SteamOS story-mode CTD fix (Ubisoft's d3d9 is buggy under Proton; DXVK shim overrides it), DXVK v3.0.1 pinned; (3) launcher-bypass wrapper (waitforexitandrun swap, same as TWFC); (4) graphics.ini preset -> Width 1920 Height 1200 (16:10) + AntiAliasing 1 + RenderQuality 2, placed in the prefix's Documents/Ubisoft/Driver San Francisco (optional step, needs the prefix). graphics.ini fields confirmed from Tony's own synced prefix; AntiAliasing is a 0/1 toggle. ASPECT-RATIO note: the game stretches 16:9->16:10; the proper fix is the PCGW aspect-ratio 'fixed executable' set (4:3/5:4/16:10, needs v1.04) but that's an exe swap and risks the same Uplay-DRM breakage the crack hit, so it's deliberately NOT bundled. Flawless Widescreen is the other option but needs a runtime app. Controller: Deck/Legion Steam Input presents an XInput pad which the game accepts; x360ce is the fallback (not bundled yet).
